name: gluetun
description: VPN gateway container for routing other apps through OpenVPN or WireGuard

fields.name: GLUETUN_STORAGE_LOCATION
fields.label: Gluetun Data Folder
fields.help: Stores Gluetun state, server data, and port-forwarding data so the container can reuse it after restart.

fields.name: VPN_SERVICE_PROVIDER
fields.label: VPN Provider
fields.help: Choose the provider you already subscribe to. The stored value matches the official Gluetun provider name.

fields.name: VPN_TYPE
fields.label: VPN Type
fields.help: Pick the connection type supported by your provider account. Many providers now prefer WireGuard.

fields.name: TZ
fields.label: Timezone
fields.help:
Timezone for logs and scheduled refresh behavior.
Example: Asia/Dubai.

fields.name: OPENVPN_USER
fields.label: OpenVPN Username
fields.help:
Needed when your provider uses OpenVPN username/password authentication.
For NordVPN, use your service credentials, not your website login.

fields.name: OPENVPN_PASSWORD
fields.label: OpenVPN Password
fields.help: Needed together with the OpenVPN username when your provider uses OpenVPN authentication.

fields.name: WIREGUARD_PRIVATE_KEY
fields.label: WireGuard Private Key
fields.help: Needed when your provider uses WireGuard. Paste the private key exactly as given by your provider.

fields.name: WIREGUARD_ADDRESSES
fields.label: WireGuard Addresses
fields.help:
Some providers also require WireGuard client addresses.
Example: 10.64.222.21/32.

fields.name: SERVER_COUNTRIES
fields.label: Server Countries
fields.help:
Optional comma-separated country filter.
Example: Netherlands,Switzerland.

fields.name: SERVER_REGIONS
fields.label: Server Regions
fields.help: Optional comma-separated region filter.

fields.name: SERVER_CITIES
fields.label: Server Cities
fields.help: Optional comma-separated city filter.

fields.name: SERVER_HOSTNAMES
fields.label: Server Hostnames
fields.help:
Optional comma-separated hostname filter.
Use only if you know the exact server names you want.

fields.name: RESTART_POLICY
fields.label: Restart Policy
fields.help: Controls whether the container restarts automatically.

fields.name: PORT_FORWARD_ONLY
fields.label: Port Forwarding Servers Only
fields.help: Restrict matching to providers or servers that support port forwarding.

fields.name: DOT
fields.label: DNS over TLS
fields.help: Encrypts DNS queries made by Gluetun.

fields.name: BLOCK_MALICIOUS
fields.label: Block Malicious Domains
fields.help: Enable Gluetun DNS filtering for known malicious domains.

fields.name: BLOCK_SURVEILLANCE
fields.label: Block Surveillance Domains
fields.help: Optional DNS filtering for telemetry and surveillance domains.

fields.name: BLOCK_ADS
fields.label: Block Ad Domains
fields.help: Optional DNS filtering for ad domains.

fields.name: GLUETUN_PUBLISHED_PORTS
fields.label: Published Ports
fields.help:
Optional YAML list of ports to publish on the Gluetun container.
Useful later for apps routed through Gluetun, such as Transmission.
Example:
- "9091:9091"
- "51413:51413"
- "51413:51413/udp"

fields.name: VPN_PORT_FORWARDING
fields.label: VPN Port Forwarding
fields.help: Enable provider-supported VPN server port forwarding.

fields.name: FREE_ONLY
fields.label: Proton Free Servers Only
fields.help: ProtonVPN only. Restrict matching to free servers.

fields.name: STREAM_ONLY
fields.label: Proton Streaming Servers Only
fields.help: ProtonVPN only. Restrict matching to streaming-optimized servers.

fields.name: SECURE_CORE_ONLY
fields.label: Proton Secure Core Only
fields.help: ProtonVPN only. Restrict matching to Secure Core servers.

fields.name: TOR_ONLY
fields.label: Proton Tor Servers Only
fields.help: ProtonVPN only. Restrict matching to Tor-over-VPN servers.

fields.name: GLUETUN_CONTAINER_NAME
fields.label: Container Name
fields.help: EasyDocker sets the container name from the project name.

fields.name: GLUETUN_IMAGE
fields.label: Image
fields.help: Recipe-managed Gluetun image.

fields.name: GLUETUN_STORAGE_TARGET
fields.label: Data Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal path where Gluetun stores state and server data.

fields.name: GLUETUN_CAP_ADD_NET_ADMIN
fields.label: Linux Capability: NET_ADMIN
fields.help: Required capability for Gluetun to manage VPN networking.

fields.name: GLUETUN_TUN_DEVICE
fields.label: TUN Device Mapping
fields.help: Provides access to the Linux TUN device required by the VPN tunnel.

ui.overview: Gluetun is a VPN gateway container that connects to your VPN provider and can later be reused by other apps that need to run through that VPN tunnel.
what_happens: A Gluetun container will be created
It will run with NET_ADMIN and access to /dev/net/tun
Its persistent state and server data will be saved in the folder you choose
You can choose your VPN provider, connection type, and optional server filters
You can optionally publish ports now for future apps that share Gluetun's network stack
ui.data_location: Relative paths are stored inside this app's project folder under the mounted EasyDocker base config path.
